query_text: Danish tech company moved headquarters to San Francisco

raw_text: Netlify, Inc. is commonly described as a San Francisco web development platform, but the company traces its founding to Copenhagen, Denmark. Co-founders Mathias Biilmann and Christian Bach started the business in 2014 after working together in the Danish startup scene. Early references describe Netlify as a Danish startup, and later company material refers to Netlify Inc. as headquartered in San Francisco, California, United States. In profiles, the company is also written simply as Netlify or Netlify Inc. For recall-oriented extraction, both the founding context and the later headquarters context matter: founded in Copenhagen, Denmark; executive headquarters in San Francisco, US. The move was tied to growth, fundraising, and customer proximity rather than a merger. Legal suffix variants such as Netlify, Inc. and Netlify Inc should be preserved.

query_text: Danish founded company moved headquarters Palo Alto Issuu
title: Issuu
snippet: Issuu was founded in Copenhagen, Denmark in 2006 and later moved its headquarters to Palo Alto, California. The digital publishing company is often listed as Issuu, Inc.
raw_text: Issuu, Inc. was founded in Copenhagen, Denmark, in 2006 by Michael Hansen, Ruben Bjerg Hansen, Mikkel Jensen, and Martin Ferro-Thomsen. The company built an electronic publishing platform and was later described as based in Palo Alto, California, United States. Historical summaries explicitly say Issuu moved its headquarters from Denmark to the United States in 2013. Some sources write the name as Issuu, while others use Issuu, Inc. or simply the formal entity Issuu Inc. Articles about the move often mention the hiring of a US chief executive and the opening of a Palo Alto office. In the same period, Issuu acquired Magma, a Denmark-based software company, so raw text may include acquisition context that should not be confused with the separate headquarters relocation signal.
